blocks:
Surah Ibrahim

Makkan, and it is 52 verses

(1) الر كِتابٌ أَنْزَلْناهُ إِلَيْكَ لِتُخْرِجَ النَّاسَ ** (1) Alif, Lām, Rā. A Book We have sent down to you that you may bring mankind by inviting them to what is in it مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ ** out of darkness from disbelief (الكفر) and types of misguidance (الضلال) إِلَى النُّورِ ** into light to faith (الإِيمان) and guidance (الهدى) بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ ** by the leave of their Lord, by His enabling (توفيقِه) and His facilitating (تسهيله) إِلى صِراطِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَمِيدِ ** to the path of the All-mighty, the All-laudable It is an appositional substitute (بدل) for His saying إِلَى النُّورِ.**

(٢) اللهِ الَّذِي لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (2. Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth.) And Allāhu was read with the rafʿ (بالرفع). وَوَيْلٌ لِلْكافِرِينَ مِنْ عَذابٍ شَدِيدٍ ** (2. Woe to the faithless because of a severe punishment,) Al-Wayl (الوَيْل) is destruction (الهلاك), the opposite of al-Wa’l (الوَأل), which is salvation (النّجاة). (٣) الَّذِينَ يَسْتَحِبُّونَ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا عَلَى الْآخِرَةِ ** (3. those who prefer the life of this world to the Hereafter,) they choose it over the latter, وَيَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَيَبْغُونَها عِوَجاً (3. and bar [others] from the way of Allah, and seek to make it crooked.) they seek crookedness (اعوجاجاً) for the way of Allah so that they may discredit it. أُولئِكَ فِي ضَلالٍ بَعِيدٍ (3. They are in manifest error.) They have strayed from the truth and fallen far away (بمراحل) from it.

(٤) وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلَّا بِلِسانِ قَوْمِهِ ** (We did not send any apostle except with the language of his people) except with the language of his people from whom he originates and among whom He sent him, so that he may clarify for them what they have been commanded, and they may understand it easily and quickly. In al-Khisal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in a hadith: "And My Lord bestowed His favor upon Me and said: 'O Muhammad, I have sent every messenger to a nation in its own language, but I have sent you to every red and black among My creation.'" فَيُضِلُّ اللهُ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** (Then Allah leads astray whomever He wishes) through abandonment (الخذلان). وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ ** (and guides whomever He wishes) through granting success (التوفيق). وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ ** (and He is the All-mighty) so He is not overcome regarding His will, the All-wise (الْحَكِيمُ) Who does not do what He does except according to His wisdom.

(٥) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا مُوسى بِآياتِنا أَنْ أَخْرِجْ قَوْمَكَ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ وَذَكِّرْهُمْ بِأَيَّامِ اللهِ ** (5) Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs, [telling him]: 'Bring your people out of darkness into light, and remind them of the Days of Allah.' It was said: [It means] His occurrences (وقائعه) that befell the past nations, and the days (أيام) of the Arabs are said to be their wars (حروبها).

And in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [It means] by the blessings (نِعَمِ) of Allah and His bounties (آلائه).

And al-Qummi [narrated]: The Days of Allah (أيام الله) are three: the Day of the Riser (يوم القائِم), the Day of Death (يوم الموت), and the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Days of Allah (أيام الله) are the Day when the Riser (القائِم) rises, the Day of the Return (يوم الكرة), and the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

I say: There is no contradiction (منافاة) among these interpretations (التفاسير), because the blessing (النعمة) upon the believer is a retribution (نقمة) upon the disbeliever. Likewise, the mentioned Days (الأيّام) are blessings (نعم) for some people and retributions (نقم) for others. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ ** (5) There are indeed signs in that for every patient and grateful person. [He is one who] is patient concerning His affliction (بلائه) and grateful for His bounties (نعمائِه).

(٦) **وَإِذْ قالَ مُوسى لِقَوْمِهِ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ أَنْجاكُمْ مِنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوءَ الْعَذابِ وَيُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْناءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِساءَكُمْ وَفِي ذلِكُمْ بَلاءٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ ** (6) And when Moses said to his people, 'Remember Allah’s blessing upon you, when He delivered you from Pharaoh’s clan, who inflicted upon you evil torment, and slaughtered your sons and spared your women. And in that there was a great trial from your Lord.'

يَسُومُونَكُمْ ** (who inflicted upon you) means: they burdened you (يكلّفونكم). سُوءَ الْعَذابِ ** (evil torment) means: their enslavement of you (استعبادكم) through difficult deeds (بالأفعال الشاقة), as previously mentioned in Surat al-Baqarah (سورة البقرة). وَيُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْناءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِساءَكُمْ وَفِي ذلِكُمْ بَلاءٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ ** (and slaughtered your sons and spared your women. And in that there was a great trial from your Lord) means: a trial (ابتلاء) from Him, or: in the deliverance (الإِنجاء) there was a blessing (نعمة).

(٧) وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكُمْ ** (And when your Lord announced) And remember when He announced that He لَئِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ ** O Children of Israel, for what I have bestowed upon you, such as the deliverance (الإنجاءِ) and other things, through faith (بالإيمان) and righteous deeds (والعَمل الصالح), لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ ** a blessing upon a blessing, وَلَئِنْ كَفَرْتُمْ إِنَّ عَذابِي لَشَدِيدٌ.** (but if you are thankless, My punishment is indeed severe.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah does not bestow a blessing upon a servant, which he recognizes with his heart and praises Allah outwardly with his tongue, completing his speech, except that an increase (بالمزيد) is commanded for him.

And in al-Majma', [there is a narration] similar in meaning. And in al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, [there is a narration] like it, and he added the Almighty's saying: لَئِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever recognizes the blessing of Allah with his heart deserves the increase (المزيد) from Allah before the expression of gratitude appears on his tongue.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah does not bestow a blessing upon a servant, whether small or great, and he says al-Hamdu Lillāh (الحمد لله), except that he has fulfilled its gratitude (أدّى شكرها). And in another narration: And the praise (الحمد) was better than that blessing. And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding the interpretation of the aspects (وجوه) of disbelief (الكفر): The third aspect of disbelief is the disbelief in blessings (كفر النعم). He said: لَئِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ وَلَئِنْ كَفَرْتُمْ إِنَّ عَذابِي لَشَدِيدٌ. (If you are grateful, I will surely increase you, but if you are thankless, My punishment is indeed severe.)

(8) وَقالَ مُوسى إِنْ تَكْفُرُوا أَنْتُمْ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً ** (And Moses said, 'If you are ungrateful, you and all those who are on the earth, [it will not harm Allah], for Allah is indeed Self-sufficient, all-laudable.') (from the two weighty groups (الثقليِنْ)) فَإِنَّ اللهَ لَغَنِيٌ from your gratitude حَمِيدٌ (all-laudable), deserving of praise in His essence (ذاته), even if no praiser praises Him. He is Praised (محمود); His Self praises Him, and the angels praise Him, and the atoms of the created things speak of His favor. Thus, by disbelief (بالكفران), you have harmed none but yourselves, since you deprived them (yourselves) of increased blessings (مزيد الأنعام) and exposed them to the severe punishment (للعذاب الشديد).

(9) أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَبَؤُا الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَعادٍ وَثَمُودَ وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ لا يَعْلَمُهُمْ إِلَّا اللهُ ** (9) Has not the news of those who were before you reached you: the people of Noah, and 'Ad and Thamud, and those after them? No one knows them except Allah. (This is) due to the multitude of their number. جاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ فَرَدُّوا أَيْدِيَهُمْ فِي أَفْواهِهِمْ ** Their apostles came to them with manifest proofs, but they put their hands to their mouths, Al-Qummi said: meaning, into the mouths of the prophets (الأنبياء). I say: It means they prevented them from speaking, and this is a metaphor (تمثيل). Regarding the exegesis of this phrase (الكلمة), there are other interpretations (وجوه أخر) mentioned by the exegetes (المفسّرون). وَقالُوا إِنَّا كَفَرْنا بِما أُرْسِلْتُمْ بِهِ وَإِنَّا لَفِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا تَدْعُونَنا إِلَيْهِ مُرِيبٍ. and said, 'We indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with, and we are indeed in grave doubt concerning that to which you invite us.'

(١٠) قالَتْ رُسُلُهُمْ أَفِي اللهِ شَكٌّ فاطِرِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يَدْعُوكُمْ لِيَغْفِرَ لَكُمْ وَيُؤَخِّرَكُمْ إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ** (10) Their apostles said, 'Is there any doubt about Allah, the originator of the heavens and the earth? He invites you to forgive you your sins, and respite you until a specified term.' Until a time which Allah named and made it the end of your lifespans. قالُوا إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُنا They said, 'You are nothing but humans like us; You have no superiority over us, so why were you singled out for prophethood instead of us? تُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَصُدُّونا عَمَّا كانَ يَعْبُدُ آباؤُنا فَأْتُونا بِسُلْطانٍ مُبِينٍ ** you desire to bar us from what our fathers used to worship. So bring us a manifest authority.' With a clear proof (حجة واضحة). They intended by that what they proposed of signs (الآيات) out of obstinacy (تعنتاً) and defiance (عناداً).

(11) قالَتْ لَهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ إِنْ نَحْنُ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ وَلكِنَّ اللهَ يَمُنُّ عَلى مَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ ** (11) Their apostles said to them, 'We are indeed nothing but human beings like you, but Allah is gracious to whomever He wishes of His servants. They acknowledged their sharing in humanity and made the cause (الموجب) for their distinction with prophethood (النّبوة) the grace (فضل) of Allah and His favor (منّه) upon them, through specific qualities (خصايص) in them that are not found in the sons of their kind (أبناءِ جنسِهِم). وَما كانَ لَنا أَنْ نَأْتِيَكُمْ بِسُلْطانٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ ** And it is not for us to bring you any authority except by Allah's leave. Meaning, the bringing of what you proposed is not up to us; rather, it is a matter related to the will (مشيّة) of Allah, so He distinguishes every prophet with a type of sign (الآيات). وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** And in Allah let the faithful put their trust. So let us put our trust (in Him) by being patient (الصَّبرِ) regarding your enmity (معاداتكم). They generalized (the statement) to indicate that which necessitates reliance (التوكل), which is faith (الإِيمان), and they intended themselves by it as a primary intention (قصداً أوّليّاً).

(١٢) وَما لَنا أَلَّا نَتَوَكَّلَ عَلَى اللهِ ** (12) 'And why should we not put our trust in Allah? Meaning: What excuse do we have for not putting our trust (in Him)? وَقَدْ هَدانا سُبُلَنا ** seeing that He has guided us in our ways? which are those by which we know Him and know that all affairs are in His hand. وَلَنَصْبِرَنَّ عَلى ما آذَيْتُمُونا وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ. We will surely bear patiently whatever annoyance you inflict on us. And in Allah let the trusting put their trust.'

(١٣) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِرُسُلِهِمْ لَنُخْرِجَنَّكُمْ مِنْ أَرْضِنا أَوْ لَتَعُودُنَّ فِي مِلَّتِنا (And the faithless said to their apostles, "We will surely expel you from our land, or you shall return to our creed.") They swore that one of the two matters must occur. And al-ʿawd (returning) is in the sense of becoming (الصّيرورة), because they had never been upon their creed (ملّة) at all. فَأَوْحى إِلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ ** meaning, to the Messengers (الرّسل) لَنُهْلِكَنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ.** (Thereupon their Lord revealed to them: "We will surely destroy the wrongdoers.)

(١٤) **وَلَنُسْكِنَنَّكُمُ الْأَرْضَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ ** (And We will surely settle you in the land after them) meaning, their land and their homes.

Al-Qummi narrated, attributed (مرفوعاً) to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever harms his neighbor out of greed for his dwelling, Allah will make him inherit his home," and He recited this Ayah.

And in al-Majma', it came in the Hadith: "Whoever harms his neighbor, Allah will make him inherit his home."

**ذلِكَ ** meaning, the destruction of the wrongdoers and the settling of the believers

لِمَنْ خافَ مَقامِي ** (That is for him who fears My station) meaning, My standing place (موقفي) for the reckoning

وَخافَ وَعِيدِ ** (and fears My threat) meaning, My threat (وعيدي) of punishment.

(15) وَاسْتَفْتَحُوا (And they sought judgment) They asked Allah for victory (الفتح) over their enemies, or judgment (القضاء) between them and their enemies, derived from al-fitāḥah (الفتاحة) meaning governance (الحكومة) [or arbitration]. وَخابَ كُلُّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ (and every obstinate tyrant failed.) In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: meaning, whoever refused to say Lā ilāha illā Allāh (لا إله إلّا الله). And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: al-ʿanīd (العنيد) (the obstinate) is the one who turns away from the truth (الحقّ).

(١٦) مِنْ وَرائِهِ جَهَنَّمُ (16) Ahead of him is hell, The fire of Hell is ahead of this tyrant (الجبّار), for he is watched over by it, standing on its brink (شفيرها) in this world, and will be sent forth to it in the Hereafter. وَيُسْقى مِنْ ماءٍ صَدِيدٍ. and he shall be given to drink of a purulent water. That is, he will be cast into it and given to drink مِنْ ماءٍ صَدِيدٍ. (of a purulent water).

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That is, وَيُسْقى (and he shall be given to drink) from what flows of blood and pus (القيح) from the private parts (فروج) of the adulteresses (الزّواني) in the Fire.

And narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), He said: It will be brought near to him, and he will detest it. When it is brought close to him, it will roast his face and the scalp (فروة رأسه) will fall off. When he drinks it, his intestines (أمعاؤه) will be cut until they exit from his anus (دبره). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَسُقُوا ماءً حَمِيماً فَقَطَّعَ أَمْعاءَهُمْ (and they will be given to drink boiling water that will cut their intestines). And He says: وَإِنْ يَسْتَغِيثُوا يُغاثُوا بِماءٍ كَالْمُهْلِ يَشْوِي الْوُجُوهَ (and if they ask for relief, they will be relieved with water like molten copper, which will scald the faces.) And al-Qummi narrated what is close to this.

(17) يَتَجَرَّعُهُ ** (He gulps it down) He forces himself to swallow it وَلا يَكادُ يُسِيغُهُ ** and he does not come close to swallowing it easily, so how can he swallow it easily? وَيَأْتِيهِ الْمَوْتُ مِنْ كُلِّ مَكانٍ ** meaning its causes, which are the hardships, so it encompasses him from all directions وَما هُوَ بِمَيِّتٍ ** yet he is not to die, so that he may find relief وَمِنْ وَرائِهِ ** and in front of him عَذابٌ غَلِيظٌ ** meaning he faces at every moment a punishment harsher than what he is currently experiencing.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his grandfather, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them): That when the Zaqqum (الزّقوم) and the Dari' (الضَّريع) boiled in the bellies of the people of the Fire like the boiling of scalding water (الحميم), they asked for a drink, and they were brought a drink of ghassaq (غسّاق) and صَدِيدٍ يَتَجَرَّعُهُ وَلا يَكادُ يُسِيغُهُ وَيَأْتِيهِ الْمَوْتُ مِنْ كُلِّ مَكانٍ وَما هُوَ بِمَيِّتٍ وَمِنْ وَرائِهِ عَذابٌ غَلِيظٌ (pus, which he gulps down and can hardly swallow it, and death comes to him from every direction, yet he is not to die, and ahead of him is a harsh punishment.) scalding water (حميم) with which Hell boils since it was created كَالْمُهْلِ يَشْوِي الْوُجُوهَ بِئْسَ الشَّرابُ وَساءَتْ مُرْتَفَقاً. (like molten copper, which scalds the faces. What a terrible drink, and what a bad resting place!)

(١٨) مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ أَعْمالُهُمْ كَرَمادٍ اشْتَدَّتْ بِهِ الرِّيحُ فِي يَوْمٍ عاصِفٍ لا يَقْدِرُونَ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا عَلى شَيْءٍ ذلِكَ هُوَ الضَّلالُ الْبَعِيدُ (18) The parable of those who disbelieve in their Lord is that their deeds are like ashes which the wind fiercely scatters on a tempestuous day. They will not be able to gain anything from what they have earned. That is the far error.

Their characteristic (صفتهم), which is a parable in its strangeness (الغرابة), is that their deeds are like ashes. The wind intensified upon it, carrying it and swiftly taking it away فِي يَوْمٍ عاصِفٍ ** (in a tempestuous day). Al-ʿasf (العصف) is the intensification of the wind. The day is described by it for emphasis (للمبالغة), just as they say, "His day is fasting (نهاره صائم)." He likened their noble deeds (مكارمهم)—such as charity (الصدقة), maintaining kinship ties (صلة الرحم), freeing slaves (عتق الرقاب), and aiding the distressed (اغاثة الملهوف)—in their nullification (حبوطها) and their vanishing as scattered dust (هباءً منثوراً), because they were built upon no foundation of the knowledge of Allah (معرفة الله) or directing them toward Him (التوجه بها إليه), to ashes scattered by the tempestuous wind (الريح العاصف). لا يَقْدِرُونَ ** (they will not be able) on the Day of Resurrection, regarding what they earned from them (the deeds), عَلى شَيْءٍ ** (to anything). Meaning, they will not see any reward (ثواباً) for any part of them. ذلِكَ ** (That)—meaning, their misguidance (ضلالهم) despite their assumption that they are doing good (محسنون)—هُوَ الضَّلالُ الْبَعِيدُ ** (is the far error), (it is) in the utmost distance from the truth (الحق).

(19) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِ ** (19) Do you not see that Allah created the heavens and the earth with the truth? with wisdom (بالحكمة) and the correct purpose (الغرض الصحيح), and He did not create them frivolously (عبثاً) or in vain (باطلاً). And it was recited (وقرئ) خالق السماوات (Creator of the heavens). إِنْ يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَأْتِ بِخَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ ** (If He wishes, He will take you away and bring a new creation.) He will annihilate you (يعدمكم) and create in your place (مكانكم) other creations (خلقاً آخرين).

(20) وَما ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ بِعَزِيزٍ ** (20) and that is not difficult for Allah. meaning: impossible (بمتعذّر) or difficult (متعسّر). (21) وَبَرَزُوا لِلَّهِ جَمِيعاً ** (21) And they will all appear before Allah. meaning: they will appear on the Day of Resurrection, and it was mentioned in the past tense (بلفظ الماضي) due to the certainty of its occurrence. فَقالَ الضُّعَفاءُ ** Then the weak will say the weak in opinion (ضعفاء الرأي), meaning the followers (الأتباع), لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا ** (to those who were arrogant), meaning their leaders (لرؤسائهم) who made them follow (استتبعوهم) and led them astray (واستغووهم). In Misbah al-Mutahajjid, in the Sermon of Ghadir by the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), after reciting it (the Ayah): "Do you know what arrogance (الاستكبار) is? It is abandoning obedience to those whom they were commanded to obey, and elevating oneself above those whom they were urged to follow." إِنَّا كُنَّا لَكُمْ تَبَعاً ** 'Indeed, we were your followers. in denying the Messengers (تكذيب الرسل) and turning away from their advice (الإِعراض عن نصائحهم). فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُغْنُونَ عَنَّا ** Will you avail us anything against us meaning: repelling from us (دافعون عنّا), مِنْ عَذابِ اللهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ قالُوا لَوْ هَدانَا اللهُ ** of Allah’s punishment?' They will say, 'Had Allah guided us, to faith (للإِيمان) and salvation from the punishment (النّجاة من العذاب). And al-Qummi said: The guidance (الهدي) here means the reward (الثّواب). لَهَدَيْناكُمْ سَواءٌ عَلَيْنا أَجَزِعْنا أَمْ صَبَرْنا ما لَنا مِنْ مَحِيصٍ ** We would have guided you. It is all the same to us whether we are restless or patient: there is no escape for us.’ meaning: a place of salvation (منجى) or refuge (مهرب) from the punishment.

(٢٢) وَقالَ الشَّيْطانُ لَمَّا قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ. (22) And Satan will say when the matter is decided: Al-Qummi said: When the affair of the world concerning his followers (أوليائه) is concluded. And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Everything in the Quran that says وَقالَ الشَّيْطانُ ** (And Satan will say) refers to the Second (الثاني). إِنَّ اللهَ وَعَدَكُمْ وَعْدَ الْحَقِ ** (Indeed Allah promised you a promise of truth), which is the Resurrection (البعث) and the recompense for deeds (الجزاءُ على الأعمال), and He fulfilled for you what He promised you. وَوَعَدْتُكُمْ ** (and I promised you) the opposite of that, but I failed you (فَأَخْلَفْتُكُمْ) and did not fulfill for you what I promised you. وَما كانَ لِي عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ سُلْطانٍ ** (I had no authority over you)—no dominance (تسلّط) by which I could compel you to disbelief (الكفر) and disobedience (العصيان)—إِلَّا أَنْ دَعَوْتُكُمْ ** (except that I called you)—except for my calling you to both of them through my enticing (تسويلي) and whispering (وسوستي)—and you responded to me (فَاسْتَجَبْتُمْ لِي), meaning you hastened your response to me. So do not blame me for my whispering (بوسوستي), for one who openly declares his enmity is not blamed for such things. وَلُومُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ ** (but blame yourselves) since you were deceived by me and obeyed me when I called you, yet you did not obey your Lord when He called you. ما أَنَا بِمُصْرِخِكُمْ ** (I cannot aid you)—meaning, I cannot rescue you (بمغيثكم) from the punishment—وَما أَنْتُمْ بِمُصْرِخِيَ ** (nor can you aid me)—meaning, you cannot rescue me (بمغيثي). None of us can save the other. Indeed, I disavow your associating me [with Allah] before. I disassociated myself from it (تبرّأت منه) and denounced it (واستنكرته), like His saying: And on the Day of Resurrection they will disavow your polytheism.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The disbelief (الكفر) in this verse means disassociation (البراءة).

إِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** (Indeed, there is a painful punishment for the wrongdoers.) (This is) either a continuation of his speech or a new commencement (استيناف).

And in the recounting of such examples, there is a kindness (لطف) for the listeners and an awakening (إيقاظ) for them, so that they may hold themselves accountable (يحاسبوا أنفسهم) and reflect upon their outcomes (عواقبهم).

(٢٣) وَأُدْخِلَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ تَحِيَّتُهُمْ فِيها سَلامٌ. (23) But those who have faith and do righteous deeds will be admitted to gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever, by the leave of their Lord. Their greeting therein will be, 'Peace!' (٢٤) أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ ضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً ** a true saying (قَولاً حقّا) and an invitation (دعاءً) to righteousness (صلاحٍ), كَشَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ whose fruit is good, like the date palm (النخلة). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: that this good tree is the date palm (النخلة). أَصْلُها ثابِتٌ ** in the earth, striking its roots (بعروقه) deep within it, وَفَرْعُها فِي السَّماءِ.

(٢٥) تُؤْتِي أُكُلَها It gives its fruit (ثمرها) كُلَّ حِينٍ every time that Allah has appointed for its fruits بِإِذْنِ رَبِّها by the will of its Creator وَيَضْرِبُ اللهُ الْأَمْثالَ لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ And Allah draws parables for mankind so that they may take admonition. Because in the drawing of parables there is a reminder and a depiction of meanings using tangible things in order to bring them closer to understanding.

And al-Ayyashi narrates from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This is a parable that Allah has drawn for the Household (أهل بيت) of His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and for those who oppose them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Tree (الشجرة) in this Ayah. He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is its root (أصلها), and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) is its branch (فرعها), and the Imams (الأئمّة), who are their descendants, are its boughs (أغصانها). The knowledge of the Imams (علم الأئمّة) is its fruit (ثمرتها), and their believing followers (شيعتهم المؤمنون) are its leaves (ورقها). He said: "By Allah, when a believer is born, a leaf sprouts on it, and when a believer dies, a leaf falls from it."

In al-Ikmal: And al-Hasan and al-Husayn are its fruit, and the nine from the progeny of al-Husayn (peace be upon them) are its boughs. And in al-Ma'ani: And the bough of the Tree is Fatimah, and its fruit are her children, and its leaves are her followers (شيعتها).

And he added in al-Ikmal: تُؤْتِي أُكُلَها كُلَّ حِينٍ ** (It yields its fruit every season) is what emerges from the knowledge of the Imam (علم الامام) to you every year from every deep ravine (فجّ عميق).

In al-Majma', al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi: [There is] what is close to these narratives. And another narrative concerning this will come in Surat Bani Isra'il regarding the Almighty's saying: **وَالشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ ** (and the cursed tree in the Qur’an), if Allah wills.

(26) وَمَثَلُ كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ ** (26) And the parable of a wicked word (is) a false saying and an invitation to misguidance and corruption, like a wicked tree whose fruit is not good, like the colocynth tree (شجرة الحنظل). اجْتُثَّتْ ** uprooted (meaning) it was completely extracted (استوصلت) and its trunk (جثته) was taken entirely, مِنْ فَوْقِ الْأَرْضِ ** from above the ground, because its roots are close to it (the surface). ما لَها مِنْ قَرارٍ ** having no stability. (meaning) stability (استقرار).

( A Narrative Discussion )

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This is a parable of Banu Umayyah.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Likewise, the disbelievers—their deeds do not ascend to the heaven. And Banu Umayyah do not remember Allah in any gathering (مجلس) nor in any mosque (مسجد), and their deeds do not ascend to the heaven, except for a few of them.

(٢٧) يُثَبِّتُ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِالْقَوْلِ الثَّابِتِ ** (27) Allah confirms those who have faith with the steadfast word, which is established by proof (الحجة) and evidence (البرهان) with them, and has become firm in their hearts, and to which their souls are reassured. فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** in the life of the world, and they remain so when they are tested in their religion. وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ ** and in the Hereafter. so they do not stammer when they are questioned about their belief (معتقدهم). وَيُضِلُّ اللهُ الظَّالِمِينَ ** And Allah leads astray the wrongdoers, those who wronged themselves through denial (الجحود) and relying solely on imitation (التقليد), so they are not guided to the truth (الحق) nor do they remain firm in situations of trial (الفتن). In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means that He leads them astray on the Day of Resurrection from the abode of His honor (دار كرامته), as will come in Surat al-Kahf regarding the Almighty's saying: وَمَنْ يُضْلِلْ ** الله فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُ وَلِيًّا مُرْشِداً وَيَفْعَلُ اللهُ ما يَشاءُ ** and whomever Allah leads astray, you will never find for him any guardian or guide. (17) And Allah does whatever He wishes, such as confirming the believers and forsaking the wrongdoers. In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Satan comes to a man from among our followers (أوليائنا) at the time of his death, from his right and his left, to lead him astray from what he is upon. But Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, refuses that for him. And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: يُثَبِّتُ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** the Ayah. And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) concerning the narration of the questioning in the grave (سؤال القبر): So the two angels say to him: "Who is your Lord, what is your religion, and who is your Prophet?" He replies: "Allah is my Lord, Islam is my religion, and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is my Prophet." So they say: "May Allah confirm you in what He loves and approves." And that is the saying of Allah: يُثَبِّتُ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** the Ayah. And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) concerning the questioning in the grave (سؤال القبر): [He said:] "And if he was a disbeliever (كافراً)..." until he said: "And Allah empowers the snakes (الحيّات) over him in his grave, biting him severely, and Satan afflicts him with distress (غمّاً)." He said: "And his torment is heard by all of Allah's creation except the jinn (الجنّ) and mankind (الإِنس). And indeed, he hears the sound of their sandals (خفق نعالهم) and the shaking of their hands (نفض أيديهم)." And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: يُثَبِّتُ اللهُ (Allah confirms...) up to His saying: وَيَفْعَلُ اللهُ ما يَشاءُ. (And Allah does whatever He wishes.) And al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi narrated what is close to these two narrations.

(٢٨) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ بَدَّلُوا نِعْمَتَ اللهِ كُفْراً وَأَحَلُّوا قَوْمَهُمْ دارَ الْبَوارِ ** (28) Have you not regarded those who changed Allah’s blessing into ingratitude, and landed their people in the house of ruin? The abode of destruction, by carrying them toward disbelief (الكفر). (٢٩) جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلَوْنَها وَبِئْسَ الْقَرارُ ** (29) Hell, which they shall enter, and it is an evil abode! And Hell is an evil resting place (المقرّ).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "What do they say concerning that?" It was said: "They say they are the two most wicked clans (الأفجران) of Quraysh: Banu Umayyah and Banu Mughirah." He said: "By Allah, it is Quraysh entirely (قاطبة). Indeed, Allah the Almighty addressed His Prophet concerning it, saying: 'I favored Quraysh over the Arabs and perfected My blessing upon them, and I sent My Messenger to them, but they بَدَّلُوا ** (changed) My blessing كُفْراً وَأَحَلُّوا قَوْمَهُمْ دارَ الْبَوارِ.** (into ingratitude, and landed their people in the house of ruin.)'"

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He meant by it Quraysh entirely (قاطبة), those who showed enmity to the Messenger of Allah and waged war against him and denied his successor (وصيّه).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): That they are the disbelievers (كفّار) of Quraysh who denied their Prophet and waged war and enmity against him.

He said: And a man asked the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) about this verse, and He said: "They are the two most wicked clans (الأفجران) of Quraysh: Banu Umayyah and Banu Mughirah. As for Banu Umayyah, they were granted enjoyment until a time (إلى حين). As for Banu Mughirah, you were spared their trouble on the Day of Badr."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the two most wicked clans (الأفجرين) of Quraysh: Banu Mughirah and Banu Umayyah. As for Banu Mughirah, Allah cut off their root (دابرهم). As for Banu Umayyah, they were granted enjoyment until a time (إلى حين). Then He said: "And by Allah, we are the blessing (نعمة الله) that Allah bestowed upon His servants, and through us, whoever achieves success (فاز) achieves it."

And in al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "What is the matter with people who changed the tradition (سنّة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and deviated from his successor (وصيّه), and they do not fear that the punishment (العذاب) will descend upon them?" Then He recited this verse. Then He said: "We are the blessing (النّعمة) that Allah bestowed upon His servants, and through us, whoever achieves success (فاز) on the Day of Resurrection achieves it."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): The end of the narration and a portion of what preceded it.

** (٣٠) **وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ أَنْداداً لِيُضِلُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ ** (30) And they set up equals to Allah, that they might lead [people] astray from His way.

Which is al-Tawhid (التوحيد). And it was recited with the opening of the yā’ (بفتح الياءِ) [i.e., لِيَضِلُّوا (that they might go astray)]. Neither leading astray (الإضلال) nor going astray (الضلال) was their purpose (غرضهم) in taking equals (الأنداد), but since it was the result (نتيجته), it was made as if it were the purpose (الغرض). قُلْ تَمَتَّعُوا (Say: 'Enjoy yourselves') is a notification (إيذان) that they were commanded to enjoy themselves (بالتمتّع) due to their immersion (لانغماسهم) in it, and that they know nothing else besides it. فَإِنَّ مَصِيرَكُمْ إِلَى النَّارِ. (for your destination is the Fire.)

(٣١) قُلْ لِعِبادِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يُقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ ** (31) Say to My servants who have faith, that they should maintain the prayer Meaning: "Maintain the prayer (أقيموا الصلوة)" (so that they maintain it (يُقِيمُوا), or "let them maintain it (لِيُقِيمُوا)"). وَيُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلانِيَةً and spend out of what We have provided them, secretly and openly. Al-Ayyashi narrated, implying (مضمراً): [This refers to] the rights (الحقوق) which are other than the obligatory Zakat (الزّكوة المفروضة). مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ يَوْمٌ لا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ ** before there comes a day in which there will be no bargaining So that the negligent person (المقصّر) may buy what he can use to remedy his negligence (تقصيره) and ransom his soul with it. وَلا خِلالٌ nor friendship. Nor close friendship (مخالّة), so that a close friend (خليل) may intercede for you. And Al-Qummi said: meaning, no charity (صدقة).

(٣٢) اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَأَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَأَخْرَجَ بِهِ مِنَ الثَّمَراتِ رِزْقاً لَكُمْ ** (32) It is Allah who created the heavens and the earth, and sent down water from the sky, and brought forth with it fruits for your sustenance, you live by it, and it includes what is eaten (المطعوم), what is worn (الملبوس), and other things besides them. وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ الْفُلْكَ لِتَجْرِيَ فِي الْبَحْرِ بِأَمْرِهِ ** and subjected to you the ships to run in the sea by His command, to wherever you direct yourselves. وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ الْأَنْهارَ and subjected to you the rivers. and He made them prepared for your utilization (لانتفاعكم) and your disposal (تصرّفكم), and He taught you the manner of utilizing them (اتخاذها).

(٣٣) وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ دائِبَيْنِ ** (33) And He subjected to you the sun and the moon, constantly pursuing their courses, [They are] in His pleasure, persisting in their course, not slackening, for the benefits of creation and the rectification of what they rectify of the earth, the vegetation, and the bodies. وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ ** and He subjected to you the night and the day. They alternate for your rest and your livelihood.

(٣٤) وَآتاكُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ ما سَأَلْتُمُوهُ. (And He gave you all that you asked Him for.) In al-Majma', narrated from them both (peace be upon them): They both recited مِنْ كُلِّ ما سَأَلْتُمُوهُ with tanwin (التنوين). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The garment (الثوب) and the thing which you did not ask Him for, He gave it to you." And perhaps the intent of بِما سَأَلْتُمُوهُ (what you asked Him for) is that which was worthy of being asked, whether it was asked or not asked. وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَةَ اللهِ لا تُحْصُوها ** (And if you enumerate Allah’s blessings, you cannot count them.) You cannot count them (لا تعدوها) nor are you able to encompass the enumeration (حصر) of their types (أنواعها), let alone their individual instances (افرادها). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): When He recited this Ayah, He would say: "Glory be to Him Who did not place in anyone the knowledge (معرفة) of a blessing except the knowledge of the shortcoming (التقصير) in knowing it, just as He did not place in anyone the knowledge of comprehending Him (إدراكه) more than the certainty (العلم) that He cannot be comprehended. So He, the Exalted, thanked the knowledge of those who know (العارفين) regarding their shortcoming in knowing how to thank Him, and thus made their knowledge of the shortcoming a form of thanks (شكراً). Just as He knew the knowledge of those who know (العالمين) that they cannot comprehend Him, so He made that knowledge faith (إيماناً), knowing from Him that He has encompassed the servants (العباد), so they do not exceed that. For nothing among His creation reaches the extent (مدى) of His worship. And how can the extent of His worship be reached by one who has no extent (مدى) and no modality (كيف)? Highly Exalted is Allah above that, a great exaltation." إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لَظَلُومٌ ** (Indeed man is a tyrannical wrongdoer,) towards the blessing (للنعمة), he does not thank it. كَفَّارٌ ** (an ungrateful denier.) He denies/rejects it (يكفرها).

(٣٥) وَإِذْ قالَ إِبْراهِيمُ رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَذَا الْبَلَدَ (35) And when Abraham said, 'My Lord, make this town — the town of Mecca — آمِناً safe, — possessing security for those within it. Its explanation has preceded in Surat al-Baqarah. وَاجْنُبْنِي وَبَنِيَّ أَنْ نَعْبُدَ الْأَصْنامَ. and keep me and my children away from worshipping idols.)

Al-Ayyashi narrates from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A man came to him and asked him about something, but He did not answer him. The man said to him: "If you are the son of your father, then you are from the descendants of the idol worshippers." He (peace be upon him) said to him: "You have lied. Indeed, Allah commanded Abraham (peace be upon him) to settle Ishmael in Mecca, and he did so. Then Abraham (peace be upon him) said: رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَذَا الْبَلَدَ آمِناً وَاجْنُبْنِي وَبَنِيَّ أَنْ نَعْبُدَ الْأَصْنامَ (My Lord, make this town safe, and keep me and my children away from worshipping idols). So, none of the descendants of Ishmael worshipped an idol. However, the Arabs were the idol worshippers, and the children of Ishmael said, 'These are our intercessors,' and they disbelieved, but they did not worship the idols."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: It has been forbidden for anyone whom disbelief has touched to assume what He entrusted to His prophets and His vicegerents, by His saying to Abraham (peace be upon him): لا يَنالُ عَهْدِي الظَّالِمِينَ (My covenant does not extend to the wrongdoers) — meaning the polytheists (المشركين), because He named polytheism (الشرك) wrongdoing (ظلماً) by His saying: إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ (Indeed polytheism is a great wrong) — So when Abraham knew that the covenant of Allah concerning the Imamate (الإمامة) does not extend to the idol worshippers, he said: وَاجْنُبْنِي وَبَنِيَّ أَنْ نَعْبُدَ الْأَصْنامَ (and keep me and my children away from worshipping idols).

In al-Amali, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, is a narration close to this one. He said at its end: "The invocation (الدعوة) concluded with me and with my brother Ali. None of us ever prostrated to an idol, so Allah took me as a Prophet (نبيّاً) and Ali as a Successor (وصيّاً)."

(٣٦) **رَبِّ إِنَّهُنَّ أَضْلَلْنَ كَثِيراً مِنَ النَّاسِ ** (36) My Lord! They have indeed led astray many among the people. They became a cause for their misguidance, like His saying: وَغَرَّتْهُمُ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا (and the life of the world deceived them). فَمَنْ تَبِعَنِي فَإِنَّهُ مِنِّي وَمَنْ عَصانِي فَإِنَّكَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ. So whoever follows me, he is indeed one of me, and whoever disobeys me—You are indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever among you fears Allah and reforms, he is one of us, the People of the House (أهل البيت)." It was asked: "One of you, the People of the House (أهل البيت)?" He said: "One of us, the People of the House (أهل البيت)." He said that Ibrahim (peace be upon him) said concerning this: فَمَنْ تَبِعَنِي فَإِنَّهُ مِنِّي. (So whoever follows me, he is indeed one of me.)

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "And whoever loves us, he is one of us, the People of the House (أهل البيت)." It was asked: "One of you?" He said: "By Allah, one of us. Have you not heard the saying of Ibrahim: فَمَنْ تَبِعَنِي فَإِنَّهُ مِنِّي. (So whoever follows me, he is indeed one of me.)"

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding: **وَمَنْ عَصانِي فَإِنَّكَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (and whoever disobeys me—You are indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful). He said: "You are able to forgive him and have mercy upon him."

(37) رَبَّنا إِنِّي أَسْكَنْتُ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي ** (37) 'Our Lord! I have settled some of my descendants Some of my children, namely Isma'il and those born from him. Al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "We are them, and we are the remainder (بقيّة) of that progeny (الذّريّة)." Al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi narrate from him (peace be upon him): "By Allah, we are the remainder (بقيّة) of that progeny (العترة)." It is added in al-Majma': "And the supplication (دعوة) of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was specifically for us." بِوادٍ غَيْرِ ذِي زَرْعٍ ** in a valley without cultivation Meaning the valley of Mecca. عِنْدَ بَيْتِكَ (1) الْمُحَرَّمِ near Your Sacred House, The Sacred (الْمُحَرَّمِ) which You have forbidden anyone to approach or treat lightly. رَبَّنا لِيُقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ ** Our Lord, that they may maintain the prayer. So make the hearts of a number of people Some of them. Al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Indeed, He did not mean all people. You are those people, and your likes. Your likeness among the people is only like the white hair on the black bull, and like the black hair on the white bull. It is incumbent upon the people to perform the pilgrimage (يحجّوا) to this House and venerate it because of Allah's veneration of it, and that they should meet us wherever we are. We are the guides (الأدلاء) to Allah." تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ ** incline toward them, Hasten towards them out of longing and affection (شوقاً ووداداً). It was recited with the opening of the waw (هَوِي), and in al-Jawami' it is attributed to the People of the House (أهل البيت) (peace be upon them), derived from هَوِيَ (like رَضِيَ) when it means 'he loved.' Its transitivity using the preposition إلى (الي) is due to the inclusion (تضمِين) of the meaning of yearning (النزوع). In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "He did not mean the House (البيت) when He said إليه (towards it), for by Allah, we are the supplication (دعوة) of Ibrahim (peace be upon him)." In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "And the hearts (الأفئدة) of the people incline towards us, and that is the supplication (دعوة) of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) when he said: فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ. (So make the hearts of a number of people incline toward them.)" In al-Basa'ir, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِي ** (So make the hearts of a number of people incline) towards us, وَارْزُقْهُمْ مِنَ الثَّمَراتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَشْكُرُونَ ** (and provide them with fruits that they may be grateful) for that blessing. So Allah answered his supplication and made it a safe sanctuary (حرماً آمناً) to which the fruits of everything are brought. Al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It means from the fruits of the hearts (ثمرات القلوب), that is, He made them beloved to the people so that they would come to them and return." In al-Ghawali, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "It is the fruits of the hearts (ثمرات القلوب)." And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The fruits are carried to them from the horizons (الآفاق), and Allah has answered him such that there is no fruit found in the lands of the East and the West that is not found there. It is even narrated that in a single day, spring, summer, autumn, and winter fruits are found there." In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), there is another hadith which preceded in Surat al-Baqarah regarding His saying: وَارْزُقْ أَهْلَهُ مِنَ الثَّمَراتِ. (and provide its people with fruits.) Al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was residing in the Syrian desert (بادية الشام). When Isma'il was born to him from Hajar, Sarah became extremely distressed because she had no child, and she used to harm Ibrahim (peace be upon him) regarding Hajar and cause him grief. Ibrahim (peace be upon him) complained of this to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. So Allah revealed to Him: "The likeness of a woman is only like the crooked rib (الضّلع العوجاء); if you leave her, you will enjoy her, but if you try to straighten her, you will break her." Then He commanded him to take Isma'il and his mother away from her. Ibrahim said: "O Lord, to which place?" He said: "To My Sanctuary (حَرَمي) and My Security (أمني), and the first piece of land I created on Earth, which is Mecca." So Allah sent down Jibra'il (peace be upon him) with the Buraq. Jibra'il carried Hajar, Isma'il, and Ibrahim (peace be upon him). Ibrahim (peace be upon him) did not pass by any beautiful place containing trees, date palms, or cultivation except that he said: "O Jibra'il, to here? To here?" Jibra'il would say: "No, proceed, proceed," until he reached Mecca. He placed them in the location of the House. Ibrahim (peace be upon him) had promised Sarah that he would not dismount until he returned to her. When they settled in that place, there were trees there. Hajar placed a cloak (كساء) she had with her upon those trees, and they sheltered beneath it. When Ibrahim sent them off (1) and settled them and intended to return to Sarah, Hajar said to him: "O Ibrahim, why do you leave us (2) in a place where there is no companion (أنيس), no water, and no cultivation?" Ibrahim (peace be upon him) said: "Allah, Who commanded me to place you in this location, is present with you." Then he departed from them. When he reached Kada (3), which is a mountain in Dhu Tuwa (4), Ibrahim (peace be upon him) turned towards them and said (peace be upon him): رَبَّنا إِنِّي أَسْكَنْتُ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي ** (Our Lord! I have settled some of my descendants) [the rest of] the Ayah. Then he proceeded, and Hajar remained. When the day advanced, Isma'il became thirsty and asked for water. Hajar stood in the valley in the place of the Sa'y and called out: "Is there any companion (أنيس) in the valley?" Isma'il (peace be upon him) disappeared from her sight. She climbed al-Safa, and the mirage (السراب) shimmered for her in the valley, and she thought it was water. So she descended into the bottom of the valley and ran (سعت). When she reached the running place (السعي), Isma'il disappeared from her. Then the mirage shimmered for her on the side of al-Safa. She descended into the valley seeking water. When Isma'il disappeared from her, she returned until she reached al-Safa. She looked until she had done that seven times. When she was on al-Marwah during the seventh circuit (الشّوط), she looked at Isma'il, and water had appeared from beneath his foot. She ran until she gathered sand around it, for it was flowing. She restrained it (فزمته) (1) with what she placed around it, and for that reason, it was named Zamzam. The tribe of Jurhum (2) was residing in Dhu al-Majaz (3) and Arafat. When the water appeared in Mecca, the birds and wild animals gathered around the water. Jurhum observed the gathering of the birds in that place and followed them until they saw a woman and a boy settled in that location, sheltering beneath a tree, and water had appeared for them. They said to Hajar: "Who are you, and what is your situation and the situation of this boy?" She said: "I am the mother of the son of Ibrahim, the Friend of the All-beneficent (خليل الرّحمن), and this is his son. Allah commanded him to settle us here." They said to her: "Will you permit us to be near you?" When Ibrahim (peace be upon him) visited them on the third day, Hajar said: "O Friend of the All-beneficent, there are people from Jurhum here who ask your permission to be near us. Do you permit them to do so?" Ibrahim (peace be upon him) said: "Yes." So Hajar permitted Jurhum, and they settled near them and pitched their tents. Hajar and Isma'il became accustomed (أنست) to them. When Ibrahim (peace be upon him) saw them on the third visit, he observed the multitude of people around them and was greatly pleased by that. The hadith is complete, and its entirety has passed in Surat al-Baqarah. Al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): When Ibrahim (peace be upon him) settled Isma'il and Hajar in Mecca and bade them farewell (وَدَعَهما) to depart from them, they wept. Ibrahim (peace be upon him) said: "Why do you weep? I have left you in the land most beloved to Allah and in the Sanctuary (حرم) of Allah." Hajar said to him: "O Ibrahim, I did not think a Prophet like you would do what you have done." He said: "And what have I done?" She said: "You have left a weak woman and a weak boy who have no means, without any human companion (أنيس), without water that appears, without cultivation that has matured, and without a udder (ضرع) to milk." Ibrahim felt compassion (فرقَّ) and his eyes watered when he heard her. He approached until he reached the door of the Sacred House of Allah (بيت الله الحرام). He took hold of the doorposts of the Ka'bah and then said: "O Allah, إِنِّي أَسْكَنْتُ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي ** (I have settled some of my descendants)" [the rest of] the Ayah. He said: Then Allah revealed to Ibrahim (peace be upon him): "Ascend Abu Qubays and call out among the people: 'O assembly of creation (معشر الخلائق), Allah commands you to perform the pilgrimage (حجّ) to this House in Mecca, which is sacred (محرّماً), مَنِ اسْتَطاعَ إِلَيْهِ ** (for those who can afford a way to it) as an obligation (فريضة) from Allah.'" So Allah extended the voice of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) until it was heard by the people of the East and the West, and what is between them, including all that Allah had decreed and ordained in the loins (أصلاب) of men from the drops (النّطف) and all that Allah had decreed and ordained in the wombs (أرحام) of women until the Day of Resurrection. It was then that the Hajj became obligatory (وجب) upon all creation, and the Talbiyah (التلبية) of the pilgrim during the days of Hajj is a response (إجابة) to the call of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) for the Hajj on that day. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He looked at the people circumambulating (يطوفون) the Ka'bah and said: "They used to circumambulate like this in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية). They were only commanded to circumambulate it and then proceed (ينفروا) to us, so they may inform us of their allegiance (ولايتنا) and affection (مودّتهم), and offer us their support (نصرتهم)." Then he recited this Ayah: فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ. (So make the hearts of a number of people incline toward them.) Al-Ayyashi added: He then said: "The Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد), the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). Then he said: 'Towards us, towards us.'"

footnote (1) The House is attributed to Him, glory be to Him, because He is its Owner; no one besides Him owns it, whereas other houses are owned by His servants. A question arises: How did He name it the House (*البيت*) when the meaning of **عِنْدَ بَيْتِكَ ** (near Your House) is that which was destined to be in Your prior knowledge? The second answer is that the House had existed before that, but *Tasm* and *Jadis* destroyed it. It is also said that Allah raised it to the heaven during the days of the Flood (*الطّوفان*). It was named the Sacred (*الْمُحَرَّمِ*) because no one can reach it except by entering the state of *Ihram*. It is also said because what is permissible in other houses is forbidden therein, such as sexual intercourse and contact with any impurities (*الأقذار*) or blood. It is also said that its meaning is the one of great sanctity (*العظيم الحُرمة*). (*Majma' al-Bayan*).

footnote (1) *سرحت فلاناً الى موضع كذا* (I sent off so-and-so to such-and-such a place) means I dispatched him. (*Sihah*).

footnote (2) *وَدَع الشّيء يدعه ودعاً* (He left the thing, *yadahu*, *wada'an*) means he abandoned it. (*M*).

footnote (3) *Kada* (*كدا*) with the long *alif* and *fathah* is the upper mountain pass (*الثنية العليا*) in Mecca near the graves. *Kuda* (*كدى*) with the *dammah* and short *alif* is the lower mountain pass (*الثنية السّفلى*) near the gate of *al-Umrah*. As for *Kudday* (*كدّي*) with the *dammah* and doubled *ya*, it is a place in the lower part of Mecca. The mention of the first two has been repeated in the *hadith*.

footnote (4) *Dhu Tuwa* (*ذو طوى*) with the *dammah* is a place in Mecca. (*S*). *Dhu Tuwa* (*ذو طوى*) with the three vowel possibilities on the *ta* and *tanwin* is a spring near Mecca. (*Q*).

footnote (1) *زمّه فانزمّ* (He restrained it, and it was restrained) means he tied it tightly. (*Q*).

footnote (2) *Jurhum* (*جرهم*), like *Qunfudh*, is a tribe from Yemen into which Isma'il (peace be upon him) married. (*Q*).

footnote (3) And *Dhu al-Majaz* (*ذو المجاز*) was a market they had, one *farsakh* from *Arafat*. (*Q*).

(٣٨) **رَبَّنا إِنَّكَ تَعْلَمُ ما نُخْفِي وَما نُعْلِنُ ** (Our Lord! Indeed You know what we conceal and what we disclose.)

You know our secret just as You know our public matters. And the meaning is that You are more knowledgeable of our states and our interests, and more merciful to us than we are to ourselves. So, we have no need for the request, but we call upon You to manifest our servitude (عبوديّة), and out of need (افتقار) for Your mercy, and seeking haste in attaining what is with You.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, knows what the servant desires when he calls upon Him, but He loves that the needs (الحوائج) be spread out to Him. So, when you supplicate, name your need.

وَما يَخْفى (١) عَلَى اللهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلا فِي السَّماءِ (and nothing is hidden from Allah in the earth or in the heaven.)

Because He is the Knower by an essential knowledge (علم ذاتي), the relation of which is equal to every known thing, and the (مِنْ) is for comprehensiveness (الاستغراق).

(٣٩) الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي وَهَبَ لِي عَلَى الْكِبَرِ ** (39) All praise belongs to Allah, who gave me, in my old age, meaning, He bestowed upon me while I was advanced in age (كبير السّنّ), despairing of having a child. He restricted the gift (الهبة) to the state of old age (حال الكبر) in order to magnify the blessing (النعمة) and to manifest the sign (الآية) contained within it. إِسْماعِيلَ وَإِسْحاقَ ** Ishmael and Isaac. It is said that Ishmael was born to him when he was ninety-nine years old, and Isaac when he was one hundred and twelve years old. إِنَّ رَبِّي لَسَمِيعُ الدُّعاءِ ** Indeed my Lord is the Hearer of prayer. meaning, He is the Responder to it, derived from your saying: "The king heard my speech," when he paid attention to it (اعتدّ به). And in this is an indication that he prayed to his Lord and asked Him for a child, and He answered him at the time when despair had set in concerning it.

(٤٠) رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلاةِ ** (40) 'My Lord! Make me a maintainer of prayer, Preparing for it, constantly observing it. وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي ** and [some] of my descendants! And some of my descendants. **رَبَّنا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعاءِ ** Our Lord! Accept my prayer! My worship.

footnote (١) انّما هو اخبار منه سبحانه بذلك وابتداء كلام من جهته لا على سبيل الحكاية عن إبراهيم بل هو اعتراض عن الجبّائي قال ثمّ عاد الى حكاية كلام إبراهيم عليه السلام فقال الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ آه م ن. (1) It is only an informing by Him, the Glorified, about that, and a commencement of speech from His side, not in the manner of relating a narration from Ibrahim. Rather, it is an objection (اعتراض) concerning al-Jubba'i. He said: Then He returned to relating the speech of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) when He said: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (al-hamdu li-Llah). آه م ن.

(٤١) **رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوالِدَيَ ** (Our Lord! Forgive me, and my parents,)

Al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two of them (عليهما السلام): Adam and Eve said it, and He read وَلِوَلَدَيَّ (and for my two children). And He attributed it in al-Jawami' (الْجَوَامِع) to the People of the House (عليهم السلام).

And Al-Qummi (said): It was only revealed as وَلِوَلَدَيَّ (for my two children), Ishmael (إِسْمَعِيل) and Isaac (إِسْحَاق).

And Al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two of them (عليهما السلام): That He used to recite رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَلَدَيَّ (Our Lord! Forgive me, and my two children), meaning Ishmael (إِسْمَعِيل) and Isaac (إِسْحَاق).

And from Al-Baqir (عليه السلام): He was asked about it, and He said: "This word was corrupted (صَحَّفَهَا) by the scribes (الْكِتَاب). His seeking forgiveness for his father was only concerning a promise He had made to him. And it was only رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَلَدَيَّ (Our Lord! Forgive me, and my two children), meaning Ishmael (إِسْمَعِيل) and Isaac (إِسْحَاق)." وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْحِسابُ ** (and the faithful, on the day when the reckoning is established.)—the Day of Resurrection (يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَة).

(٤٢) وَلا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللهَ غافِلاً عَمَّا يَعْمَلُ الظَّالِمُونَ ** (Do not suppose that Allah is oblivious of what the wrongdoers are doing.) (This is) a threat to the wrongdoer and a consolation to the oppressed. إِنَّما يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ (He only defers them) means He defers their punishment لِيَوْمٍ تَشْخَصُ فِيهِ الْأَبْصارُ. (for a day when the eyes will stare [in terror].) Al-Qummi said: Their eyes will remain open due to the terror of Hell (جهنّم); they will not be able to blink (يَطْرِفُوا).

(٤٣) مُهْطِعِينَ ** (hastening/rushing) [They are] hastening toward the Caller, or approaching with their gazes, not blinking due to awe and fear. And الإِهطاع is the act of approaching something. مُقْنِعِي رُؤُسِهِمْ ** (raising them [their heads]) (2) لا يَرْتَدُّ إِلَيْهِمْ طَرْفُهُمْ ** (their gaze does not revert to them), rather their eyes remain fixed, not blinking. وَأَفْئِدَتُهُمْ هَواءٌ (and their hearts are void). It was said [it means] خلاء (emptiness), meaning empty of intellects (العقول) due to excessive confusion (الحيرة) and astonishment (الدهشة). They possess no strength (قوّة), nor boldness (جرأة), nor understanding (فهم). And al-Qummi said: Their hearts are splitting (يتصدّع) from palpitation (الخفقان).

footnote (١) Our companions (*أصحابنا*) used this as evidence for their view that the parents of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) were not disbelievers, because he only asks forgiveness for them on the Day of Resurrection. If they had been disbelievers, he would not have asked for that, because He said: **فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ أَنَّهُ عَدُوٌّ لِلَّهِ تَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُ** (But when it became clear to him that he was an enemy of Allah, he disowned him). Thus, it is correct that the father who was a disbeliever was only his maternal grandfather or his paternal uncle, according to the difference of opinion concerning him. And whoever says that he only prayed for his father because he had promised him that he would embrace Islam, but when he died upon disbelief, he disowned him, as narrated from al-Hasan—his saying is invalid (*فاسد*). Because Ibrahim only made this supplication after old age and after Isma'il and Ishaq were granted to him. At this time, the enmity of his disbelieving father toward Allah had already become clear to him, so it is not permissible for him to intend him with his supplication. (*مجمع البيان*).

footnote (٢) Meaning: raising their heads toward the sky, so that a man cannot see the place of his foot due to the intensity of raising the head, and that is due to the terror (*هول*) of the Day of Resurrection. (*م ن*).

(٤٤) **وَأَنْذِرِ النَّاسَ (٤٤) يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمُ الْعَذابُ فَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا رَبَّنا أَخِّرْنا إِلى أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ نُجِبْ دَعْوَتَكَ وَنَتَّبِعِ الرُّسُلَ ** (44) And warn the people of the day when the punishment overtakes them, and the wrongdoers will say, 'Our Lord! Respite us for a short term, and we will respond to Your call and follow the apostles.'

O Muhammad, وَأَنْذِرِ النَّاسَ يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمُ الْعَذابُ فَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا رَبَّنا أَخِّرْنا إِلى أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ نُجِبْ دَعْوَتَكَ وَنَتَّبِعِ الرُّسُلَ ** Grant us respite until a near period of time (أَمَد من الزمان قريب) so that we may rectify what we neglected regarding responding to Your call and following Your apostles. أَوَلَمْ تَكُونُوا أَقْسَمْتُمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** [This is understood] upon the intention of the saying: 'There is no cessation (زَوال) for you.' Al-Qummi: You will not perish (لا تهلكون).

(٤٥) وَسَكَنْتُمْ فِي مَساكِنِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ ** (and you inhabited the dwellings of those who wronged themselves) through disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). وَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمْ كَيْفَ فَعَلْنا بِهِمْ and it became clear to you how We dealt with them, by what you witness in their dwellings of the traces of what descended upon them, and what reached you consecutively (تواتر) concerning their reports (اخبارهم). وَضَرَبْنا لَكُمُ الْأَمْثالَ ** (and We drew parables for you) yet you did not take heed.

(٤٦) وَقَدْ مَكَرُوا مَكْرَهُمْ ** (46) And they have indeed devised their scheme, The scheme in which they exhausted their effort to nullify the truth and establish falsehood. وَعِنْدَ اللهِ مَكْرُهُمْ ** (And with Allah is their scheme), meaning, their scheme is written down with Him, so He is going to recompense them for it. Or, He has with Him that by which He schemes against them, as a recompense for their scheme and a nullification of it. وَإِنْ كانَ مَكْرُهُمْ ** (And even if their scheme was) in greatness and severity لِتَزُولَ مِنْهُ الْجِبالُ ** (so that the mountains would vanish because of it) from their places. القمّيّ said: The scheme of Banu Fulan (a certain tribe/group). And it was read لَتَزول with the fathah on the lām and the rafʿ (on the verb).

(٤٧) فَلا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللهَ مُخْلِفَ وَعْدِهِ رُسُلَهُ ** (47) So do not suppose that Allah will break His promise to His apostles. Like His saying: إِنَّا لَنَنْصُرُ رُسُلَنا كَتَبَ اللهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَرُسُلِي إِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ ** (Indeed, We will surely help Our apostles. Allah has written: 'I shall surely overcome, I and My apostles.' Indeed, Allah is All-mighty) Dominant (غالبٌ). ذُو انتِقامٍ ** for His allies (لأوليائه) against His enemies (من أعدائه).

(٤٨) يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ الْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ الْأَرْضِ وَالسَّماواتُ (The day the earth is changed into another earth, and the heavens [as well]) Meaning: And the heavens are changed into other heavens. Narrated through the way of the ʿĀmmah (general populace) from Ali (peace be upon him): [They will be] an earth of silver and heavens of gold. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [It is] a pure loaf (خبزة نقيّة) from which people eat until they finish the reckoning (الحساب). It was said: "Indeed, people on that day will be too preoccupied to eat and drink." He (peace be upon him) said to them: "In the Fire, they are not preoccupied from eating al-ḍarīʿ (الضَّريع) and drinking al-ḥamīm (الحَميم) while they are in the torment. So how can they be preoccupied from it during the reckoning (الحساب)?" And in another narration: "Indeed, Allah created the son of Adam hollow (أجوف); he must have food and drink. Are they more intensely preoccupied on that day, or those who are in the Fire, who have already sought relief, and Allah says: وَإِنْ يَسْتَغِيثُوا يُغاثُوا بِماءٍ كَالْمُهْلِ يَشْوِي الْوُجُوهَ بِئْسَ الشَّرابُ. (And if they ask for relief, they will be relieved with water like molten copper, which will scald the faces. What a terrible drink!)" And in al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to these two. And narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him) regarding: تُبَدَّلُ الْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ الْأَرْضِ (the earth is changed into another earth). He (peace be upon him) said: "Meaning, an earth upon which sins have not been earned (لم تكسب عليهَا الذنوب), exposed, upon which there are neither mountains nor vegetation, just as He spread it out (دحاها) the first time." In al-Majma', narrated through the way of the ʿĀmmah (general populace) from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Allah will change the earth into another earth, and He will spread it out and extend it like the extension of the ʿUkāẓī (العكاظي) hide (1). You will see in it neither crookedness (عوجاً) nor unevenness (امتاً). Then Allah will rebuke the creation with a single rebuke (زجرة), and behold, they will be in this changed [earth] in positions similar to their positions in the first [earth]: what was in its belly will be in its belly, and what was on its surface will be on its surface." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "People will be gathered on the Day of Resurrection upon a white, dusty (عفراء) earth (2), like a pure loaf (قرصة النقيّ), in which there is no distinguishing mark (معلم) for anyone." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and it was said to him: "Where will the creation be then?" He replied: "They are the guests of Allah, and what He possesses will not fail them." In al-Iḥtijāj, narrated from him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked about this Ayah, and it was said to him: "Where will the people be on that day?" He replied: "In the darkness (الظلمة) before the place of gathering (المحشر)." And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Those who love one another for the sake of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, on the Day of Resurrection will be upon an earth of green emerald (زبرجدة خضراء), in the shade of His Throne, on His right side—and both His hands are right." In al-Khiṣāl and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Indeed, since Allah created the earth, He has created seven worlds (عوالم) upon it, none of whom are descendants of Adam. He created them from the surface (أديم) of the earth, and they inhabited it one after the other, along with their world (عالمه). Then Allah created Adam, the father of this humanity, and created his progeny from him. And by Allah, Paradise has not been devoid of the souls of the believers since He created it. Perhaps you think that when the Day of Resurrection comes, and Allah places the bodies of the people of Paradise with their souls in Paradise, and places the bodies of the people of the Fire with their souls in the Fire, that Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, will not be worshipped in His lands, and will not create a creation that worships Him, affirms His Oneness (يوحّدونه), and glorifies Him? Yes, indeed! He will surely create a creation without males (فحولة) or females (إناث) who will worship Him, affirm His Oneness, and glorify Him. And He will create for them an earth to carry them and a heaven to shade them. Does not Allah say: يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ الْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ الْأَرْضِ وَالسَّماواتُ (The day the earth is changed into another earth, and the heavens [as well]) And Allah said: أَفَعَيِينا بِالْخَلْقِ الْأَوَّلِ بَلْ هُمْ فِي لَبْسٍ مِنْ خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ (Did We fail in the first creation? Rather, they are in doubt about a new creation.)" وَبَرَزُوا لِلَّهِ الْواحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ (And they shall come forth before Allah, the One, the All-subduing) For His reckoning (لمحاسبته) and His recompense (ومجازاته).

(1) ʿUkāẓ (عكاظ), like (غراب), is a market (سوق) in a desert between Nakhla (نخلة) and al-Ṭāʾif (الطّائف). It used to be held at the new moon of Dhū al-Qaʿdah (ذي القعدة) and would last for twenty days. The Arab tribes would gather and yataʿākaẓūn (يتعاكظون), meaning they would boast and recite poetry to one another. From this comes the ʿUkāẓī (العكاظي) hide (الأديم). Q.

(2) Al-ʿAfrah (العفرة) is a whiteness that is not brilliant (النّاصع), but rather the color of the dust (عفر) of the earth, which is its surface (وجهها). From this comes the ḥadīth: "People will be gathered on the Day of Resurrection upon a white, dusty (عفراء) earth." Nihāyah.

(٤٩) وَتَرَى الْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُقَرَّنِينَ فِي الْأَصْفادِ. (49) And you will see the guilty on that day bound together in fetters. Al-Qummi said: [They are] bound (مقيّدين) some of them to others. It was said: Perhaps this is according to their participation in beliefs (العقائد), morals (الأخلاق), and deeds (الأعمال). (٥٠) سَرابِيلُهُمْ Their shirts (قمصانهم) مِنْ قَطِرانٍ And it is what is smeared upon mangy camels, burning the mange and the skin. It is black and foul-smelling, and fire ignites in it quickly. And it was recited (وقرئ) as مِنْ قِطْرانٍ (min qiṭrān). And al-qaṭr (القطر) is molten copper (النّحاس) and brass (الصُّفر), whose heat is extreme (المتناهي حرّه). وَتَغْشى وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ ** Their shirts will be of pitch, and the Fire will cover their faces. He specified the faces because the face is the most precious and noblest place on the exterior of the body, just as the heart is in its interior. For this reason, He said: تَطَّلِعُ عَلَى الْأَفْئِدَةِ (It will rise over the hearts). And because they did not turn their faces toward the Truth (الحق) and did not use their senses (مشاعرهم) and faculties (حواسّهم)—which were created for this purpose—in contemplating Him, just as [the Fire] rises over their hearts (أفئدتهم) because they are empty of knowledge (المعرفة) and filled with ignorance (الجهالات). Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding: سَرابِيلُهُمْ مِنْ قَطِرانٍ ** (Their shirts will be of pitch). He said: It is the hot, molten brass (الصّفر). Allah says: Its heat has reached its limit (انتهى حرّه). وَتَغْشى وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ ** (and the Fire will cover their faces). They were clad in that brass, so تَغْشى وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ. (the Fire will cover their faces). And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) said: Gabriel (جبرئيل) said: "If a single shirt (سربالاً) from the shirts (سرابيل) of the people of the Fire were hung between the heaven and the earth, the people of the earth would die from its stench (ريحه) and its blaze (وهجه)." And in Nahj al-Balagha: And He clothed them in shirts (سرابيل) of pitch (القطران) and garments (مقطّعات) of fire, in a torment whose heat has intensified, and a door that has been sealed upon its inhabitants.

(٥١) لِيَجْزِيَ اللهُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ ** (51) that Allah may requite every soul meaning, He does that to them so that He may requite every soul ما كَسَبَتْ إِنَّ اللهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ ** (for what it has earned. Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning.) because the reckoning of one does not distract Him from the reckoning of another, and its explanation has preceded in Surat al-Baqarah.

(٥٢) هذا بَلاغٌ لِلنَّاسِ ** (52) This is a proclamation for mankind, sufficient for them in admonition so that they may be advised وَلِيُنْذَرُوا بِهِ وَلِيَعْلَمُوا أَنَّما هُوَ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ ** (that they may be warned by it, and that they may know that He is the One God,) through reflection and contemplation upon it, وَلِيَذَّكَّرَ أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ (and that those who possess intellect may take admonition)—those who possess minds (العقول) and sound judgment (النهى).

And al-Qummi (said regarding) هذا بَلاغٌ لِلنَّاسِ : it means Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

في ثواب الأعمال In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Whoever recites Surat Ibrahim عليه السلام and al-Hijr together in two ركعتين every Friday, poverty shall never afflict him, nor madness, nor affliction (بلوى), if Allah wills."
